mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, it signals a severe labor market deterioration that triggers recession risk premiums, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 7.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending across durable and non-durable sectors, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a t-statistic below 1.96 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production during periods of tight supply constraints signals impending output bottlenecks and inflationary pressure, forcing risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum during supply-constrained regimes yields a positive 5-day forward return in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS momentum remains below 0.02 across all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230.0, it confirms entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL crosses above 230.0 (p-value > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent quarterly decline in real GDP and a monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals a broad-based economic contraction that reduces aggregate demand and corporate profits, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining GDPC1 and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (t-stat < 1.64).

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 95.0, it indicates a sharp contraction in manufacturing and mining output that triggers short-term risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where INDPRO drops below 95.0.
